Subscribers onlyThe embarrassment of the French wine industry amid Russian tensions                Since the outbreak of the war in Ukraine, Russian-based brands and consultants have been keeping a low profile and awaiting a more peaceful future.

Russia strikes Kyiv as U.N. leader visits

Subscribers onlyIn Sudan, West Darfur is the scene of more massacres  At least 213 people have been killed in four days of violence in Darfur, according to authorities in the western Sudan region. The clashes were condemned by the United Nations, which on Wednesday called for a 'rapid' and 'independent' investigation.

Subscribers onlyHit by high inflation, French small businesses see their margins shrink  As raw material and energy prices soar, thousands of companies are caught between greedy suppliers and dissatisfied customers.

Paris suspends electric bus fleet after two fires  Public transport operator RATP announced that 149 electric buses will be taken off the streets of Paris temporarily 'as a precaution' after two of the vehicles caught fire.

Feature Subscribers only'A girl I knew was killed in Odesa with her 3-month-old daughter and her mother': The diary of two sisters separated by the war in Ukraine In France, Olga took part in a concert with her choir and is on her way to Vienna to celebrate Orthodox Easter with a friend. In Kyiv, Sasha is slowly getting her old social life back and is getting ready to celebrate Easter with her mother and... Viktor. The sisters agreed to keep a diary of their daily life during the ninth week of the war in Ukraine.   10 min read

Interview   Isabelle Regnier Subscribers only'For Ukrainian heritage, the scale of the disaster is already colossal' In an interview with 'Le Monde,' Lazare Eloundou Assomo (head of UNESCO's World Heritage Center) said that, in addition to human losses, Russia's war is causing considerable damage to classified sites.   5 min read

Investigation   Pascale Santi Subscribers onlyIncreasing suicide rates among French young people worry health professionals The latest data shows rising suicides among minors, mainly girls. This situation is all the more worrying because healthcare services are overloaded. Throughout France, child health professionals are calling for a collective effort.   11 min read

Investigation   Antoine Albertini and  Simon Piel Subscribers onlyFrench mayor charged with complicity in drug trafficking maintains her innocence Mélanie Boulanger, the mayor of a small town near Rouen, has been charged along with one of her deputies for 'complicity in drug trafficking.' 'Le Monde' reveals the story behind the indictment.   12 min read
